tests.name: Doxycycline Hyclate - Content (Assay)
tests.limit: 95.0 per cent to 102.0 per cent of C22H25ClN2O8 (anhydrous and ethanol-free substance)
tests.details: Liquid chromatography (2.2.29) as described in the test for related substances with the following modification: Injection: test solution and reference solution (a). Calculate the percentage content of C22H25ClN2O8 taking into account the assigned content of doxycycline hyclate CRS.

tests.name: Doxycycline Hyclate - Identification A (HPLC)
tests.limit: The principal peak in the chromatogram obtained with the test solution is similar in retention time to the principal peak in the chromatogram obtained with reference solution (a).
tests.details: Examine the chromatograms obtained in the assay. Reference solution (a) is Doxycycline hyclate CRS.

tests.name: Doxycycline Hyclate - Identification B (Sulfuric Acid)
tests.details: To about 2 mg add 5 mL of sulfuric acid R.

tests.name: Doxycycline Hyclate - Identification C (Chlorides)
tests.details: Reaction (a) of chlorides (2.3.1).

tests.name: Doxycycline Hyclate - pH
tests.details: Dissolve 0.1 g in carbon dioxide-free water R and dilute to 10 mL with the same solvent.

tests.name: Doxycycline Hyclate - Absorbance at 490 nm
tests.limit: maximum 0.07 at 490 nm (anhydrous and ethanol-free substance)
tests.details: Dissolve 0.10 g in a mixture of 1 volume of a 103 g/L solution of hydrochloric acid R and 99 volumes of methanol R and dilute to 10.0 mL with the same mixture of solvents. Carry out the measurement within 1 h of preparing the solution.

tests.name: Doxycycline Hyclate - Related Substances (Impurity A)
tests.details: Liquid chromatography (2.2.29). Prepare solutions immediately before use. Solution A: Suspend 111.6 g of sodium edetate R in 900 mL of water for chromatography R. Adjust to pH 7.0 with concentrated ammonia R to achieve complete dissolution, then dilute to 1 L with water for chromatography R. Test solution: Dissolve 20.0 mg of the substance to be examined in a 1 g/L solution of hydrochloric acid R and dilute to 25.0 mL with the same solution. Reference solution (a): Dissolve 20.0 mg of doxycycline hyclate CRS in a 1 g/L solution of hydrochloric acid R and dilute to 25.0 mL with the same solution. Reference solution (b): Dissolve 5 mg of doxycycline for system suitability CRS (containing impurities A, B, C and F) in a 1 g/L solution of hydrochloric acid R and dilute to 10 mL with the same solution. Reference solution (c): Dilute 1.0 mL of the test solution to 100.0 mL with a 1 g/L solution of hydrochloric acid R. Column: size l=0.25 m, Ø=4.6 mm; stationary phase: end-capped polar-embedded octadecylsilyl amorphous organosilica polymer R (5 μm); temperature: 35 °C. Mobile phase: mix 13 volumes of acetonitrile R, 17 volumes of water for chromatography R, 35 volumes of a 67.9 g/L solution of tetrabutylammonium hydrogen sulfate R previously adjusted to pH 7.0 with concentrated ammonia R and 35 volumes of solution A. Flow rate: 1.0 mL/min. Detection: spectrophotometer at 280 nm. Injection: 20 μL of the test solution and reference solutions (b) and (c). Run time: twice the retention time of doxycycline. Identification of impurities: use the chromatogram supplied with doxycycline for system suitability CRS and the chromatogram obtained with reference solution (b) to identify the peaks due to impurities A, B, C and F. Relative retention with reference to doxycycline (retention time = about 21 min): impurity C = about 0.4; impurity A = about 0.7; impurity B = about 0.8; impurity F = about 1.3. System suitability: reference solution (b), resolution: minimum 2.0 between the peaks due to impurities A and B; minimum 2.0 between the peaks due to impurity B and doxycycline. Calculation of percentage contents: for each impurity, use the concentration of doxycycline hyclate in reference solution (c).

tests.name: Doxycycline Hyclate - Related Substances (Impurity F)
tests.details: Liquid chromatography (2.2.29). (Refer to details for Doxycycline Hyclate - Related Substances (Impurity A) for full method description.)

tests.name: Doxycycline Hyclate - Related Substances (Impurity B)
tests.details: Liquid chromatography (2.2.29). (Refer to details for Doxycycline Hyclate - Related Substances (Impurity A) for full method description.)

tests.name: Doxycycline Hyclate - Related Substances (Impurity C)
tests.details: Liquid chromatography (2.2.29). (Refer to details for Doxycycline Hyclate - Related Substances (Impurity A) for full method description.)

tests.name: Doxycycline Hyclate - Related Substances (Unspecified impurities)
tests.limit: for each impurity, maximum 0.10 per cent
tests.details: Liquid chromatography (2.2.29). Reporting threshold: 0.05 per cent. (Refer to details for Doxycycline Hyclate - Related Substances (Impurity A) for full method description.)

tests.name: Doxycycline Hyclate - Related Substances (Total impurities)
tests.details: Liquid chromatography (2.2.29). (Refer to details for Doxycycline Hyclate - Related Substances (Impurity A) for full method description.)

tests.name: Doxycycline Hyclate - Ethanol
tests.details: Gas chromatography (2.2.28). Internal standard solution: Dilute 0.50 mL of propanol R to 1.0 L with water R. Test solution: Dissolve 0.10 g of the substance to be examined in the internal standard solution and dilute to 10.0 mL with the same solution. Reference solution: Dilute 0.5 mL of anhydrous ethanol R to 100.0 mL with the internal standard solution. Dilute 1.0 mL of this solution to 10.0 mL with the internal standard solution. Column: material: fused silica; size: l=50 m, Ø=0.32 mm; stationary phase: methylpolysiloxane R (film thickness 5 μm). Carrier gas: helium for chromatography R. Linear velocity: 37 cm/s. Split ratio: 1:10. Temperature: Column 0-7 min at 70°C, 7-13 min from 70°C to 160°C, 13-15 min at 160°C. Injection port 200°C. Detector 250°C. Detection: flame ionisation. Injection: 1 μL. Calculate the content of ethanol, taking the density (2.2.5) at 20 °C to be 0.790 g/mL.

tests.name: Doxycycline Hyclate - Water
tests.details: Water (2.5.12), determined on 1.20 g.

tests.name: Doxycycline Hyclate - Sulfated ash
tests.details: Sulfated ash (2.4.14), determined on 1.0 g.

tests.name: Doxycycline Hyclate - Bacterial endotoxins
tests.details: Bacterial endotoxins (2.6.14), if intended for use in the manufacture of parenteral preparations without a further appropriate procedure for the removal of bacterial endotoxins.

tests.name: Doxycycline Monohydrate - Content (Assay)
tests.limit: 95.0 per cent to 102.0 per cent of C22H24N2O8 (anhydrous substance)
tests.details: Liquid chromatography (2.2.29) as described in the test for related substances with the following modification: Injection: test solution and reference solution (a). Calculate the percentage content of C22H24N2O8 taking into account the assigned content of doxycycline hyclate CRS.

tests.name: Doxycycline Monohydrate - Identification A (HPLC)
tests.limit: The principal peak in the chromatogram obtained with the test solution is similar in retention time to the principal peak in the chromatogram obtained with reference solution (a).
tests.details: Examine the chromatograms obtained in the assay. Reference solution (a) is Doxycycline hyclate CRS.

tests.name: Doxycycline Monohydrate - Identification B (Sulfuric Acid)
tests.details: To about 2 mg add 5 mL of sulfuric acid R.

tests.name: Doxycycline Monohydrate - Identification C (Water)
tests.details: Refers to the Water content test (2.5.12).

tests.name: Doxycycline Monohydrate - pH
tests.details: Suspend 0.1 g in carbon dioxide-free water R and dilute to 10 mL with the same solvent.

tests.name: Doxycycline Monohydrate - Absorbance at 490 nm
tests.details: Dissolve 0.10 g in a mixture of 0.5 volumes of hydrochloric acid R and 99.5 volumes of methanol R and dilute to 10.0 mL with the same mixture of solvents. Carry out the measurement within 1 h of preparing the solution.

tests.name: Doxycycline Monohydrate - Related Substances (Impurity A)
tests.details: Liquid chromatography (2.2.29). Prepare solutions immediately before use. Solution A: Suspend 111.6 g of sodium edetate R in 900 mL of water R. Adjust to pH 7.0 with concentrated ammonia R to achieve complete dissolution, then dilute to 1 L with water R. Test solution: Dissolve 20.0 mg of the substance to be examined in a 1 g/L solution of hydrochloric acid R and dilute to 25.0 mL with the same solution. Reference solution (a): Dissolve 20.0 mg of doxycycline hyclate CRS in a 1 g/L solution of hydrochloric acid R and dilute to 25.0 mL with the same solution. Column: size l=0.25 m, Ø=4.6 mm; stationary phase: end-capped polar-embedded octadecylsilyl amorphous organosilica polymer R (5 μm); temperature: 35 °C. Mobile phase: mix 13 volumes of acetonitrile R, 17 volumes of water for chromatography R, 35 volumes of a 67.9 g/L solution of tetrabutylammonium hydrogen sulfate R previously adjusted to pH 7.0 with concentrated ammonia R and 35 volumes of solution A. Flow rate: 1.0 mL/min. Detection: spectrophotometer at 280 nm. Injection: 20 μL of the test solution and reference solutions (b) and (c) (as defined in Doxycycline Hyclate monograph). Run time: twice the retention time of doxycycline. Identification of impurities and System suitability same as Doxycycline Hyclate. Calculation of percentage contents: for each impurity, use the concentration of doxycycline hyclate in reference solution (c).
